ST. MARY'S NURSING CENTER INC

CCN: 215013 · LEONARDTOWN, MD 20650 · St. Marys County

Overview

Address
21585 PEABODY STREET, LEONARDTOWN, MD 20650
Phone
3014758000
Certified beds
160
Avg daily residents
116 (73% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1967-01-01
Setting
Urban
5 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 5/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 5/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.70 3.74 3.81 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.57 0.65 0.59 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.91 0.83 0.94 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.22 2.26 2.29 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.48 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.04 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.19
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.30
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
3.29
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
42.6%
annual
RN turnover
16.7%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.33
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.96
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2024-11-22 6 6 1 28 1 28
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2019-08-27 10 5 5 44 1 44
Total weighted health score
32.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (18)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

1 A/B/C No actual harm — minor
17 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-06
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-11-22 2025-01-06
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-06
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-06
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-06
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2024-11-22 2025-01-06
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-09
0624 Prepare residents for a safe transfer or discharge from the nursing home. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-09
0626 Permit a resident to return to the nursing home after hospitalization or therapeutic leave that exceeds bed-hold policy. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-09
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-09
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2024-07-26 2024-08-09
0577 Allow residents to easily view the nursing home's survey results and communicate with advocate agencies. C 2019-08-27 2019-10-10
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. E 2019-08-27 2019-10-10
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2019-08-27 2019-10-10
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. E 2019-08-27 2019-10-10
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2019-08-27 2019-10-10
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2018-06-06 2018-07-21
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2018-06-06 2018-07-21
